chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: Define speed and write its formula.
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time. Speed = Distance ÷ Time.

questions.question: Differentiate between rest and motion.
questions.answer: An object is at rest if its position does not change with time, while it is in motion if its position changes with time.

questions.question: State the SI unit of speed.
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What is uniform motion? Give one example.
questions.answer: Uniform motion is when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time. Example: a car moving at constant speed.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion? Give one example.
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time. Example: a car moving in city traffic.

questions.question: What is the function of a stopwatch?
questions.answer: A stopwatch is used to measure short intervals of time accurately.

questions.question: What is periodic motion? Give one example.
questions.answer: Periodic motion is motion that repeats at regular intervals of time, such as the motion of a pendulum.

questions.question: What is oscillatory motion?
questions.answer: Oscillatory motion is the back-and-forth motion of an object about a fixed position.

questions.question: What does a speedometer measure?
questions.answer: A speedometer measures the speed of a vehicle.

questions.question: What does an odometer measure?
questions.answer: An odometer measures the total distance travelled by a vehicle.

questions.question: What is the relationship between distance, speed, and time?
questions.answer: Distance = Speed × Time.

questions.question: What type of motion is shown by the hands of a clock?
questions.answer: The hands of a clock show circular motion.

questions.question: What is the time period of motion?
questions.answer: Time period is the time taken to complete one full cycle of periodic motion.

questions.question: Name the instrument used to measure distance travelled by vehicles.
questions.answer: An odometer is used to measure the distance travelled by vehicles.

questions.question: What does a straight line represent in a distance–time graph?
questions.answer: A straight line represents uniform motion.

questions.question: What does a curved line in a distance–time graph indicate?
questions.answer: A curved line indicates non-uniform motion.

questions.question: Give an example of oscillatory motion.
questions.answer: The swinging of a pendulum is an example of oscillatory motion.

questions.question: Why is time important in studying motion?
questions.answer: Time helps determine how fast an object moves and allows calculation of speed.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of distance?
questions.answer: The SI unit of distance is metre (m).

questions.question: Name one device used for measuring longer intervals of time.
questions.answer: A clock is used for measuring longer intervals of time.

questions.question: What kind of motion does a moving car usually have in city traffic?
questions.answer: A car in city traffic usually shows non-uniform motion.

questions.question: Give one example of circular motion in daily life.
questions.answer: The motion of the blades of a fan is an example of circular motion.

questions.question: What happens to speed if distance increases while time remains constant?
questions.answer: The speed increases.

questions.question: What happens to speed if time increases while distance remains constant?
questions.answer: The speed decreases.

questions.question: Name one natural example of periodic motion.
questions.answer: The motion of the Earth around the Sun is an example of periodic motion.
